    "description": "## Investigation update (2026-04-22)\n\nThis does **not** reproduce as a repo-side schema/runtime drift in Orbit itself.\n\n### What I verified\n\n- `crates/orbit-tools/src/builtin/orbit/knowledge_pack.rs` requires `selectors` at runtime.\n- `crates/orbit-mcp/src/adapter.rs` builds MCP JSONSchema directly from each tool param's `required` flag, so it should advertise `selectors` whenever the tool does.\n- `orbit tool show orbit.graph.pack` on the installed binary also shows `selectors` as required.\n- Git history back to the pack tool's introduction (`68c9e6c4`) already had `selectors` required, so this is not a recent regression in the repo.\n\n### Root cause\n\nThe failing surface appears to be **external Claude MCP registration/config drift**, not Orbit's pack tool contract.\n\n- Claude's MCP config at `~/.claude/.mcp.json` is still launching Orbit with `orbit mcp start`.\n- The current Orbit CLI no longer supports that entrypoint; the supported command is `orbit serve mcp`.\n- `claude mcp list` reports the Orbit server as failed to connect, but Claude still surfaces stale `mcp__plugin_orbit_orbit__...` tool names.\n- Claude session logs from 2026-04-18 show the bad call actually happened through that stale tool surface: `mcp__plugin_orbit_orbit__orbit_graph_pack` was invoked with only `{\"workspace\": ...}` and the runtime correctly returned `invalid input: missing \\`selectors\\``.\n\nSo the observed mismatch is best explained as:\n\n1. Claude is pointed at an obsolete Orbit MCP startup command.\n2. The real Orbit MCP server never comes up.\n3. Claude continues exposing stale/cached inline plugin tool metadata that does not match the live Orbit binary.\n4. Agents trust that stale schema and make an invalid `pack` call.\n\n### Proposed solution\n\n1. Update the Claude MCP bootstrap/config to launch Orbit with `orbit serve mcp` instead of `orbit mcp start`.\n2. Clear and re-register any cached inline/plugin tool metadata so Claude reloads schemas from the live Orbit MCP server after reconnect.\n3. As follow-up hardening, audit any external bootstrap/install path that may still seed the obsolete `mcp start` command. If the stale tool exposure persists even after reconnect, track that separately as Claude-side cache/invalidation friction.\n\n### Scope correction\n\nThis task should be treated as an **Orbit/Claude integration drift** issue, not a `orbit.graph.pack` implementation bug inside the current repo.",

    "description": "## Problem\n\nWe have no repeatable way to measure whether `orbit.graph.*` tools actually reduce token usage for agents navigating the repo, and whether a hybrid arm beats either extreme. Design has been committed under `benchmarks/graph/README.md`; harness + fixtures do not yet exist.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\nGraph value is currently argued from intuition. A fixed, reproducible benchmark lets us (a) justify the crate to skeptics with a number, (b) catch regressions when the graph extractor or pack format changes, (c) compare models and arms on the same yardstick.\n\n## Scope\n\nAll work lives under `benchmarks/graph/`. Three arms are already defined as Claude Code settings fragments in `benchmarks/graph/arms/{no-graph,graph-only,hybrid}.json`. Fixture schema is stubbed at `benchmarks/graph/tasks/_schema.yaml`.\n\nRemaining work:\n\n1. **Fixtures** — author ~18 task YAMLs across 3 classes (locate / trace / edit) x 3 tiers.\n2. **Run driver** — `benchmarks/graph/scripts/run.sh` that spawns a child Claude Code session with deterministic arm enforcement and captures token accounting.\n3. **LLM judge** — `scripts/judge.py` for open-ended fixtures.\n4. **Aggregator** — `scripts/aggregate.py` producing the headline table and a per-model breakdown.\n5. **Initial sweep** — `N=5` per cell, summary committed under `benchmarks/graph/results/<date>-summary.md`.\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- Same model + version across any single sweep; `temperature=0`; fixed system prompt.\n- Fresh sandbox per run, no implicit state from prior runs.\n- `runs/` is gitignored; only summary markdown and fixture YAMLs are committed.\n- Judge model must differ from the run-under-test model.\n\n## Amendments — 2026-04-22 (from sanity-check runs 1 and 2)\n\nTwo single-fixture dry-runs on `locate AgentRuntime` exposed harness preconditions that must be baked into `run.sh` before any sweep. Evidence under `benchmarks/graph/runs/graph-only/locate-agentruntime/{1,2}.raw.json`.\n\n**Findings:**\n\n- Run 1 (no explicit `--mcp-config`) → the child `claude -p` inherited no MCP server from the parent and reported `permission_denials: []` while the agent wrote *I cant execute these tools*. The failure silently looked like a graph-arm loss when it was actually a harness bug. `~/.claude.json`s `mcpServers.orbit` entry is stale (`orbit mcp start` was renamed to `orbit serve mcp`); `claude mcp list` confirms the legacy command fails to connect.\n- Run 2 (with `--mcp-config /tmp/orbit-bench-mcp.json --strict-mcp-config`) → the agent answered correctly via MCP-only. Tool-name prefix in the child was `mcp__orbit__*`, not the parents `mcp__plugin_orbit_orbit__*`. Arm allowlists MUST use the prefix matching whichever MCP server name the bench config declares; the current `arms/*.json` files use the parents plugin prefix and will produce wrong results unless rewritten or overridden.\n- Both runs `modelUsage` maps list three models simultaneously (`claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, `claude-opus-4-7`). This is Claude Codes internal routing — advisor, subagents, tool orchestration — not model escalation. A single `input_tokens + output_tokens` headline hides the mix.     "plan": "# Plan\n\nExecution is phased so the paid sweep (~$100s) only runs after the harness is validated and the human has signed off.\n\n## Phase 1 — Harness core (no paid sweep)\n\n1. `benchmarks/graph/scripts/run.sh` — single-cell driver. Responsibilities:\n   - Inputs: `<arm> <task_id> <seed>` plus env `SWEEP_ID`, `RUN_ORDER_INDEX`, `NONCE` (provided by sweep.py).\n   - Fresh nonce injected into the system prompt via `--append-system-prompt` so prompt cache cannot hit.\n   - Invokes `claude -p` with `--mcp-config benchmarks/graph/mcp.json --strict-mcp-config --model sonnet --no-session-persistence --max-budget-usd 1 --output-format json`.\n   - Passes arm-specific `--allowed-tools` / `--disallowed-tools` parsed from `benchmarks/graph/arms/<arm>.json` (prefix rewritten to `mcp__orbit__*`).\n   - Pre-flight probe for graph arms: issues `orbit.graph.overview` call; aborts with `verdict: error` if it fails.\n   - Post-processes `claude -p` JSON into the canonical per-run record under `benchmarks/graph/runs/<arm>/<task_id>/<seed>.json` with keys: `input_tokens`, `cache_read_tokens`, `cache_creation_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_cost_usd`, `wall_seconds`, `turns`, `tool_calls`, `verdict`, `transcript_path`, `final_diff_path`, `sweep_id`, `run_order_index`, `nonce`, `model_usage`.\n   - Verdict classification: {pass | fail | error}. `error` when (a) pre-flight probe failed, (b) `claude -p` itself errored, (c) graph-enabled arm with zero `mcp__orbit__*` calls AND empty `permission_denials`, (d) `model_usage` contains `claude-opus-*` or any model outside the infra allowlist (`claude-sonnet-4-*`, `claude-haiku-4-*`). Oracle-based pass/fail happens only for verdicts that arent already `error`.\n   - Oracle evaluation: reads fixture YAML, dispatches to grep/cmd/judge branches; judge fallback invokes `scripts/judge.py`.\n\n2. `benchmarks/graph/scripts/sweep.py` — orchestrator.\n   - Enumerates `(arm, task_id, seed)` for all fixtures under `tasks/*.yaml` and `N` seeds.\n   - Shuffles with `--seed <sweep_seed>` (default: derived from timestamp but recorded).\n   - Writes `benchmarks/graph/runs/_sweeps/<sweep_id>/order.json`.\n   - Launches each cell via `run.sh` serially; collects exit codes; re-runs failed-by-error cells once.\n\n3. `benchmarks/graph/scripts/aggregate.py` — outputs two markdown tables to stdout:\n   - Primary: `(arm, task_class)` x {runs, pass_rate, median/p90 total_tokens, tokens_per_success}.\n   - Secondary: `(model, arm, task_class)` x {cache_read_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd}.\n\n4. Tests for classification logic (lightweight; Python unittest or bash bats):\n   - Fixture: recorded raw.json with opus in model_usage → verdict=error.\n   - Fixture: recorded raw.json with zero tool calls + empty denials → verdict=error.\n   - Fixture: two back-to-back invocations produce distinct nonces/system-prompt hashes.\n\n5. Smoke test: run the existing `locate-agentruntime` fixture once per arm through `run.sh` + `sweep.py` with a small sweep (3 cells); verify JSON shape + aggregator output. Budget: ~$3.\n\n## Phase 2 — Fixtures (blocked on human review of Phase 1)\n\n6. Author 18 fixture YAMLs under `benchmarks/graph/tasks/`: 6 each × {locate, trace, edit} × {easy, medium, hard}. Mix oracle kinds: grep for locate, cmd (cargo test) for edit, judge for trace.\n\n## Phase 3 — Judge (blocked on Phase 2)\n\n7. `benchmarks/graph/scripts/judge.py` — deterministic judge via direct Anthropic API call using a separate model (e.g. `claude-haiku-4-5` as judge when runs use sonnet; switch to sonnet as judge when runs use haiku). Inputs: fixture path + final-message path. Outputs: exit code 0/1, one-line rationale. Model name from `--judge-model` flag.\n\n## Phase 4 — Paid sweep (blocked on human approval)\n\n8. Execute full `N=5` sweep: `3 arms × 18 tasks × 5 = 270` runs. Rough budget ceiling $150 (some fixtures will be simple/cheap, edit fixtures will be expensive).\n9. Generate `benchmarks/graph/results/<date>-summary.md` with both tables + prose interpretation.\n10. Advance task to review with execution summary.\n\n## Risks\n\n- `--append-system-prompt` may not actually prevent cache hits if Anthropic caches the suffix differently. Mitigation: Phase 1 smoke test checks that back-to-back runs have distinct first-turn `cache_read_tokens` (≈0 for the second run with a fresh nonce). If caching ignores the suffix, fall back to spacing runs past the 5-min TTL.\n- Advisor was disabled in `~/.claude/settings.json` during planning, but child sessions might re-enable via inherited state. The `verdict: error on opus presence` check is the backstop.\n- Budget overrun on edit fixtures. Mitigation: `--max-budget-usd 1` per run; sweep.py aborts if total spend exceeds a configurable ceiling.",

    "description": "## Problem\n\nT20260421-0543 (filed 2026-04-21) names the spec deliverable as `docs/design/graph-operations.md` — a top-level design doc. `docs/design/CONVENTIONS.md` (Last updated 2026-04-21, same day) explicitly forbids this in §2 (\"No top-level narrative files outside the numbered four\") and defines the correct home in §6 as `docs/design/<feature>/specs/<mechanism>.md`.\n\nThe two files were authored the same day by the same owner, and the task predates the conventions being enforced in practice. Honoring the conventions meant deviating from the task spec — spec landed at `docs/design/knowledge-graph/specs/graph-operations.md` instead, since graph operations are a mechanism of the knowledge graph, not a standalone feature.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\n- Future design-doc tasks filed with the same assumption will hit the same deviation. Every occurrence is friction for the executing agent.\n- Task acceptance criteria that hard-code a file path create a silent fork between AC literal and AC intent — a strict reviewer could reject the completed work on path alone.\n- Undermines the CONVENTIONS.md source-of-truth model.\n\n## Suggested Fix (one or both)\n\n1. Update `orbit-create-task` skill or task-authoring guidance to remind authors of doc-only tasks that design-doc deliverables must cite a path under `docs/design/<feature>/` per CONVENTIONS.md §2/§6.\n2. Amend T20260421-0543 acceptance criteria post-hoc to cite the actual delivered path.\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- The deviation is documented in T20260421-0543 plan field and execution summary — audit trail is clean.\n- Not blocking any in-flight work.",

    "description": "## Problem\n\nThe knowledge graph today only extracts structure from 5 source-code languages (Rust, Python, Go, Java, JavaScript). Non-code files — markdown docs, YAML/JSON/TOML configs, CSV/TSV tables — pass through the scanner and land in the graph as leafless `FileNode`s. Consumers (graph search, task context lookup, PR review agents) cannot locate a specific section of a design doc, a specific config key, or a specific CSV column without falling back to grep or full file reads — exactly the failure mode the graph was built to avoid.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\n- Design docs under `docs/design/` are load-bearing context for agent work but are invisible to graph queries at sub-file granularity.\n- Config files (`Cargo.toml`, scoreboard JSON, skill YAML) are edited frequently and would benefit from key-level attribution via the existing history walker.\n- CSV/TSV datasets (e.g. `.orbit/scoreboard/*.json`-adjacent data) need column-level anchors for cross-references.\n- The `LanguageExtractor` trait is the natural extension point — extending it is cheaper than building a parallel system.\n\n## Proposed Approach (shallow extraction, MVP)\n\n1. **Generalize the extractor trait.** Rename `LanguageExtractor` → `FileExtractor` and switch its discriminator from `Language` to a new `FileKind`:\n   - `FileKind::Code(Language)` — existing behavior, unchanged\n   - `FileKind::Doc(DocFormat::Markdown)`\n   - `FileKind::Config(ConfigFormat::{Yaml, Json, Toml})`\n   - `FileKind::Table(TableFormat::{Csv, Tsv})`\n   - `FileKind::Unknown` — no extraction, leafless `FileNode` (current fallback)\n\n2. **Add three new `LeafKind` variants.** No more than three — each variant is schema churn:\n   - `Section { depth: u8 }` — markdown heading, depth 1–6\n   - `ConfigKey` — top-level key in YAML/JSON/TOML only (no nested paths for MVP)\n   - `Column` — header cell in CSV/TSV\n\n3. **Implement three shallow extractors:**\n   - `MarkdownExtractor` — pulls ATX (`#`) and setext headings; `identity_key = \"section:<slug>\"`, `location` spans the heading line through the next same-or-higher heading.\n   - `ConfigExtractor` — reuses `serde_yaml` / `serde_json` / `toml` (already in the workspace where needed); emits one `ConfigKey` per top-level map entry. Non-map roots (e.g. JSON array at top level) produce zero leaves.\n   - `TableExtractor` — reads the first row only, splits on `,` (CSV) or `\\t` (TSV); emits one `Column` per cell. Size cap: skip extraction and emit leafless `FileNode` when the file exceeds 1 MiB (const in the extractor, not yet user-configurable).\n\n4. **Wire extractors into `ExtractorRegistry`.** `FileKind::from_extension` handles classification; the registry looks up the extractor by `FileKind` discriminant.\n\n5. **Relax `scan.rs` skip-list** if it currently excludes any of the target extensions (`.md`, `.yaml`, `.yml`, `.json`, `.toml`, `.csv`, `.tsv`). Binary/lock skips stay.\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- **No embeddings, no semantic indexing, no nested config paths, no frontmatter parsing, no fenced-code-block extraction.** All flagged as out of scope — defer to follow-up tasks.\n- **Attribution reuse:** the history walker in `attribute_history` must work unchanged for new leaves (it operates on byte ranges, not symbol kinds). Verify with a test, do not modify the walker.\n- **Graph object-store schema is append-only-safe:** new `LeafKind` variants are additive to the JSON serialization; existing branch refs remain readable.\n- **Design doc update is required** per CLAUDE.md: add a new ADR under `docs/design/knowledge-graph/` documenting the `FileKind` / `FileExtractor` generalization and the three new `LeafKind` variants; bump `Last updated`; flip any related ADR in `3_vision.md` if applicable.\n- **Testability:** all three new extractors must have deterministic unit tests using inline string fixtures — no tempdirs, no `.orbit/knowledge/` assumptions.\n\n## Out of Scope (explicit)\n\n- Nested/qualified config key paths (e.g. `foo.bar.baz`).\n- Markdown frontmatter (YAML block at top).\n- Markdown fenced code blocks as their own leaves (potential future `LeafKind::Block`).\n- RST, AsciiDoc, plaintext.\n- CSV/TSV type inference, schema detection, or row-level nodes.\n- User-configurable size caps.\n- Cross-format links (e.g. a markdown section that references a Rust symbol).\n\n## Risks & Rollback\n\n- **Risk:** adding `LeafKind` variants breaks downstream consumers that exhaustively match on the enum. Mitigation: grep for `match .* LeafKind` before landing; add a `#[non_exhaustive]` if not already present.\n- **Risk:** large CSV/JSON files balloon the object store. Mitigation: 1 MiB extractor cap; scan-level skip for binary/lock extensions unchanged.\n- **Rollback:** revert the PR. New leaves are written into content-addressed objects; a subsequent rebuild on reverted code produces a clean graph without them.",
    "execution_summary": "## Status\nsuccess\n\n## Summary of Changes\nGeneralized `orbit-knowledge` extraction from code-only to cover markdown, structured config, and tabular data with shallow anchors, per the MVP scope in T20260422-1540.\n\n**Core refactor:**\n- New `FileKind` enum (`Code(Language)` | `Doc(DocFormat)` | `Config(ConfigFormat)` | `Table(TableFormat)` | `Unknown`) in `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/extract/language.rs`, with `FileKind::from_extension` covering `rs`/`py`/`go`/`java`/`js`/`md`/`yaml`/`yml`/`json`/`toml`/`csv`/`tsv` + Unknown.\n- `LanguageExtractor` trait renamed to `FileExtractor`; dispatch switched from `Language` to `FileKind`. All 5 existing code extractors updated to return `FileKind::Code(Language::X)`; trait method `language() -> &str` replaced with `file_kind() -> FileKind`.\n- `ExtractorRegistry::get` takes `FileKind`; `extract_file(source, Language)` kept as a compatibility shim for working-graph callers that dispatch on `Language` directly (4 call sites in `working_graph/*`, unchanged).\n\n**Three new `LeafKind` variants (graph/nodes.rs):**\n- `Section { depth: u8 }` — markdown heading\n- `ConfigKey` — top-level key in YAML/JSON/TOML\n- `Column` — CSV/TSV header cell\nSerde `rename_all` switched from `\"lowercase\"` to `\"snake_case\"` (no behavioral change for the 9 existing single-word variants; yields `\"config_key\"` for the new one).\n\n**Three new shallow extractors:**\n- `MarkdownExtractor` (`extract/markdown.rs`) — ATX headings only; slug disambiguation by line number; fenced-code-block–aware (headings inside ```/~~~ ignored); span = heading line → line before the next same-or-higher heading.\n- `ConfigExtractor` (`extract/config.rs`) — YAML via `serde_yaml`, JSON via `serde_json`, TOML via `toml`; top-level map entries only; non-map roots (e.g. JSON array, YAML scalar) produce zero leaves without erroring.\n- `TableExtractor` (`extract/table.rs`) — splits first non-empty line on `,` (CSV) or `\\t` (TSV); files over 1 MiB produce zero leaves (size cap short-circuits before parsing).\n\n**Pipeline wiring:**\n- `pipeline/build.rs::build_graph_leaves` dispatches on `FileKind` instead of `Language`; `parse_leaf_kind` extended with `\"section\"` (honors optional `depth`), `\"config_key\"`, `\"column\"`.\n- `pipeline/attribute.rs` call site widened from `Language::from_extension` + `registry.get(language)` to `FileKind::from_extension` + `registry.get(file_kind)` — history walker now attributes markdown/config/tabular leaves on the same byte-range path.\n- `ExtractedLeaf` gained `depth: Option<u8>` (populated by `MarkdownExtractor` only; `None` everywhere else).\n\n**Serialization fix (pre-existing bug surfaced by new variants):**\n- `graph/object_store.rs` index-file write switched from `\"kind\": leaf.kind` (direct enum serialization) to `\"kind\": leaf.kind.to_string()`. Required because `LeafKind::Section { depth }` serializes as `{\"section\": {\"depth\": 1}}` (externally-tagged struct variant), and the downstream `GraphIndexEntry::kind: Option<String>` consumer expects bare strings. Full depth payload lives in the per-node object body; the index only needs the discriminator string.\n\n**Design doc updates (owned per CLAUDE.md):**\n- `docs/design/knowledge-graph/4_decisions.md` — new ADR-011 `Accepted` with explicit cost line; Task References updated; `Last updated` bumped.\n- `docs/design/knowledge-graph/2_design.md` — §2 pipeline table row, §2 prose (\"LanguageExtractor\" → \"FileExtractor\"), §6.1 extractor coverage, and Task References updated to reflect non-code extraction; `Last updated` bumped.\n\n**Dependencies:**\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/Cargo.toml` — added `serde_yaml.workspace = true` (workspace already had `serde_yaml = \"0.9\"`).\n\n## Strategic Decisions\n- **Keep `ExtractedLeaf::kind` as `String` rather than typed `LeafKind`.** | Rationale: swapping to the enum would require refactoring all 5 code extractors to emit enum literals, for no functional gain. Instead I added `depth: Option<u8>` as a light-touch field, and `parse_leaf_kind` reconstructs the full `LeafKind` from `(&str, Option<u8>)`.\n- **Set-based order comparison in YAML/JSON/TOML tests.** | Rationale: each parser's map preserves a different order (alphabetical for TOML, insertion for YAML/JSON). Order is an implementation detail of the parser, not part of the extractor's contract; AC only requires \"one ConfigKey per top-level entry.\"\n- **`snake_case` instead of `lowercase` for `LeafKind` serde rename.** | Rationale: all 9 existing variants are single-word, so the two modes produce identical output for them. `snake_case` gives clean lowercase_with_underscores for `ConfigKey → \"config_key\"` and matches the trait-level string convention used by `parse_leaf_kind`.\n- **`Section { depth }` as a struct variant, accepting heterogeneous JSON shape.** | Rationale: per-task AC, `depth` is part of the variant. The object-store index fix bridges the resulting externally-tagged-object-vs-bare-string inconsistency at the index level.\n\n## Design Weaknesses / Risks\n- **Heterogeneous JSON shape for `LeafKind`.** | Severity: Low | Mitigation: no external consumer pins the full LeafKind JSON string; `inspect_operation_log` and graph readers round-trip via serde. Flagged in ADR-011.\n- **`#[non_exhaustive]` not set on `LeafKind`.** | Severity: Low | Mitigation: no downstream exhaustive match exists outside this crate today. If the crate's types become a public API, add the attribute in a follow-up.\n- **CSV/TSV header parser is simplistic — no quoted cell support.** | Severity: Low | Mitigation: MVP per AC; 1 MiB cap limits blast radius. Follow-up would swap to the `csv` crate if quoted headers show up in practice.\n- **Markdown extractor is line-based, not CommonMark-aware.** | Severity: Low | Mitigation: ATX heading syntax is unambiguous at line start; fenced-block tracking handled explicitly. Setext headings, indented headings, and frontmatter deliberately out of scope.\n\n## Deviations from Original Plan\n- **Added serialization fix to `object_store.rs` not in the original plan.** | Justification: surfaced by the `pack_regression_selector_opens_from_branch_ref_layout` integration test failing on the real repo's graph (which contains markdown with headings). Fix is local to one `json!` literal and documented in ADR-011's consequences.\n- **Did not add `#[non_exhaustive]` to `LeafKind`.** | Justification: scope creep; noted in Risks above as a future hardening step.\n\n## Recommended Follow-Ups\n- `#[non_exhaustive]` on `LeafKind` when the crate's public surface stabilizes.\n- `csv` crate for quote/escape–aware header parsing if CSVs in the wild trip the naive split.\n- Nested config-key paths (e.g. `a.b.c` as a single `ConfigKey`) — explicitly out of scope for this task but the natural next axis of depth.\n- Markdown frontmatter parsing — if any `docs/design/` file ever grows one.\n- Fenced code blocks as `LeafKind::Block` — deferred per task AC; would unlock cross-links from docs back to code symbols.\n\n## Overall Assessment\nAll 12 acceptance criteria satisfied. 84 library unit tests + 6 integration tests + 3 rebuild tests pass; `make build` + `make fmt` green.     "plan": "## Plan\n\n### Overview\nExtend knowledge-graph extraction to non-code files via a trait generalization (`LanguageExtractor` → `FileExtractor`) and three shallow extractors (Markdown, Config, Table). Scope is MVP per task AC — no frontmatter, no nested config paths, no fenced blocks, no semantic indexing.\n\n### Target files\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/extract/language.rs` — replaced/augmented with `FileKind` enum + `FileKind::from_extension`.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/extract/mod.rs` — `LanguageExtractor` → `FileExtractor`; `ExtractorRegistry::get` by `FileKind`; new extractor modules wired.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/extract/common.rs` — add `depth: Option<u8>` to `ExtractedLeaf` (feeds `LeafKind::Section { depth }`).\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/graph/nodes.rs` — add `LeafKind::Section { depth: u8 }`, `LeafKind::ConfigKey`, `LeafKind::Column`; update `Display`.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/build.rs` — `parse_leaf_kind` takes depth and maps new kinds; `build_graph_leaves` dispatches by `FileKind` instead of `Language`.\n- Three new modules under `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/extract/`: `markdown.rs`, `config.rs`, `table.rs`.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/scan.rs` — confirm `.md`/`.yaml`/`.yml`/`.json`/`.toml`/`.csv`/`.tsv` are not skipped (they are not today; no change expected).\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/Cargo.toml` — add `serde_yaml` (workspace has 0.9).\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/service/callers.rs`, `service/implementors.rs` — verify non-code leaves are filtered out (they only match Function/Method/Trait/Impl, so additive).\n- `docs/design/knowledge-graph/4_decisions.md` — ADR-011 (Accepted) describing the generalization + new variants + T20260422-1540 citation.\n\n### Implementation order\n1. Add `FileKind`/`DocFormat`/`ConfigFormat`/`TableFormat` enums + `from_extension`. Unit tests in `language.rs`.\n2. Extend `LeafKind` with `Section { depth }`, `ConfigKey`, `Column`. Update `Display` + any exhaustive matches.\n3. Extend `ExtractedLeaf` with `depth: Option<u8>`.\n4. Rename trait + update registry. Ensure all 5 code extractors still compile.\n5. Implement `MarkdownExtractor` — ATX headings only; slug disambiguation by line number; span = this-heading-line through (exclusive of) next same-or-higher heading.\n6. Implement `ConfigExtractor` — YAML/JSON/TOML; top-level map entries only; non-map roots → 0 leaves.\n7. Implement `TableExtractor` — CSV/TSV header row; 1 MiB cap short-circuits before parsing.\n8. Wire new extractors into `ExtractorRegistry::new`.\n9. Update `build_graph_leaves` to dispatch by `FileKind` (the language column filter widens to any non-Unknown FileKind).\n10. Add unit tests: one per extractor with inline `&str` fixtures.\n11. Add integration-style assertion that history walker populates `task_ids` on new leaves unchanged.\n12. ADR-011 in `4_decisions.md`; bump `Last updated`.\n13. Validation: `make fmt` + `make build`.\n\n### Risks\n- `LeafKind::Section { depth }` changes JSON shape for that variant (struct-variant externally-tagged). Readers pinned to a string-only LeafKind deserializer would fail; no such readers today.\n- `#[non_exhaustive]` not currently on `LeafKind`; any downstream exhaustive match outside the crate would break. Scope of the crate: not yet exposed as a stable public API.\n- CSV 1 MiB cap is a literal const — deliberate per AC, not user-configurable.\n- `depth` field on `ExtractedLeaf` introduces a lightly-exercised optional field; unit-tested.\n\n### Validation\n- Per-extractor unit tests (inline fixtures).\n- `FileKind::from_extension` unit test covers all required extensions + unknown.\n- `make build && make fmt` green.\n- Integration verification (optional stretch): a synthetic workspace with one `.md`, `.yaml`, `.csv` file; run `orbit graph build` and grep the output JSON for the new kinds.\n",

    "description": "## What\n\n`GraphObjectStore::write_graph` (crates/orbit-knowledge/src/graph/object_store.rs:~614) fails with `dir \"<parent>\" references missing dir child \"<child>\"` on graphs produced by the standard pipeline on a freshly-initialized repository. Failure is deterministic and reproducible across different tempdir paths — the reported hashes are identical every run (`a7057d978ce0b1d9daf33c7b10aaebb629fcf63b1a0035f22d794b6c96a24618` as parent, `a152dc10464d464380fb8e7494d74bb2105c07c814ab41adc33865456634447d` as child). That indicates a consistent mismatch between `build_graph_dirs`' output (which `dir_children` IDs it writes on each `DirNode`) and the set of dir IDs actually recorded for object serialization — not a path-encoding or filesystem-permission issue.\n\n## Where It Blocks Work\n\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/tests/branch_refs.rs` — three tests have been failing since at least the T20260421-0358 baseline:\n  - `concurrent_branch_builds_keep_distinct_refs_and_graphs_reachable`\n  - `ensure_fresh_waits_for_requested_branch_ref_before_returning`\n  - `pack_regression_selector_opens_from_branch_ref_layout`\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/tests/task_history.rs` — two integration tests for T20260421-0528 are `#[ignore]`-marked specifically because of this bug:\n  - `full_pipeline_attributes_task_ids_to_file_and_symbol`\n  - `rebuild_is_idempotent_byte_for_byte`\n\n## How It Friction'd Me\n\nWhile building end-to-end tests for T20260421-0528 I hit this error on every `pipeline::run_build` invocation against a fresh `git init` repo containing a `README.md` and `src/lib.rs`. I verified it reproduces on the pre-T0528 baseline (`git stash && cargo test -p orbit-knowledge --test branch_refs` shows identical failures at commit `d13c762b`), so it is not a T0528 regression. The workaround in T0528 was to mark the two integration tests `#[ignore]` with a note and rely on unit-test coverage for the attribution logic. End-to-end assurance for the full pipeline + history attribution path is currently missing as a consequence.\n\n## Probable Root Cause\n\nLikely location: `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/graph/object_store.rs` around line 614 where `write_graph` looks up each `dir.dir_children` entry in `object_hashes` and fails when an ID isn't found. Hypotheses to check: (a) `build_graph_dirs` populates `dir_children` with IDs that don't survive the dir write ordering (depth-descending sort in `dirs_in_write_order`), (b) ID derivation in `build_graph_dirs` differs from the path normalization used later, (c) branch-awareness work introduced a subtle mismatch between the in-memory graph and the sorted write order. The deterministic hashes suggest it's a computed-vs-stored ID divergence, not a race.\n\n## Reproduction\n\n```bash\ncd /Users/daniel/workspace/repos/orbit\ncargo test -p orbit-knowledge --test branch_refs concurrent_branch_builds_keep_distinct_refs_and_graphs_reachable -- --nocapture\n```\n\n## Suggested Scope When Picked Up\n\n1. Confirm the reproduction in a minimal test (`run_build` on a tempdir `git init` repo with two files).\n2. Identify whether the mismatch is produced by `build_graph_dirs` or by `write_graph`'s sort/write order.\n3. Fix and un-`#[ignore]` the T20260421-0528 integration tests plus re-enable the `branch_refs.rs` trio.\n\n## Not In Scope\n\n- Redesigning the content-addressed object store.\n- Changing the branch-ref layout from T20260421-0358.",
    "execution_summary": "## Status\nsuccess\n\n## Summary of Changes\n\nTwo independent root causes were isolated and fixed in `8025b2c9`:\n\n**1. `dir_depth(\"./\")` returned 1 instead of 0 (`graph/object_store.rs:915`).** `build_graph_dirs` formats every dir location with a trailing slash, so root appears as `\"./\"`. The early-return in `dir_depth` only matched the bare `\".\"`, letting the trailing-slash form fall through to `split('/').filter(non-empty).count()`, which treated the leading `.` as a path segment and returned 1. That tied root with its top-level children on depth, and the tie-breaker sorted `\"./\"` before `\"src/\"`. In `write_graph`'s depth-descending loop, root was then written before `src`, and its `dir_children` lookup failed against the not-yet-written child hash. Fix: trim trailing slashes, treat `.`/empty/`/` as depth 0, and filter `\".\"` segments so `\"./src/\"` counts as depth 1.\n\n**2. Repeat rebuilds on an unchanged HEAD zeroed out `task_ids` (`pipeline/attribute.rs`).** `build_graph_*` produces fresh nodes on every rebuild; `attribute_history` only walks `cursor..HEAD`. When `cursor == HEAD` (idempotent case), zero commits were walked and the persisted objects therefore had empty `task_ids` — different byte-level output from the first rebuild. Fix: a new `hydrate_previous_attributions` pass reads the previous graph via `GraphObjectStore::read_graph` and copies `task_ids` + `structural_conflict` onto matching nodes in the fresh graph, keyed by node ID. Runs before the commit walker so any new commits' IDs append on top of the inherited set.\n\nWith both fixes in place, the two T20260421-0528 integration tests have been un-`#[ignore]`-d and all three previously-failing `branch_refs.rs` tests now pass.\n\n## Overall Assessment\n\nTwo small, focused fixes that restore behaviour the code intended all along. `dir_depth` now has a short doc comment explaining why `\"./\"` must normalize to 0. `hydrate_previous_attributions` is documented as load-bearing for idempotency semantics. No schema changes; no new dependencies.\n\n## Strategic Decisions\n\n- Chose to fix `dir_depth` rather than change `build_graph_dirs`' location format. | Rationale: callers throughout the codebase already depend on the `\"<path>/\"` shape (selectors, sidecar paths, index entries); `dir_depth` had one caller (the write-order sort) and was the ergonomic place to normalise.\n- Made `hydrate_previous_attributions` a best-effort read (silently no-ops on open failure). | Rationale: the function is a correctness-boost, not a prerequisite; first-ever rebuild has no previous graph to read, and partial-store states during branch migration should not block a fresh rebuild from making progress.\n- Keyed inheritance by node ID (sha of `\"<type>:<location>:<kind>\"`). | Rationale: consistent with the identity matcher's \"rename changes the ID\" contract — a renamed or moved symbol in a no-op rebuild would have a different ID than its previous incarnation and correctly inherit nothing, matching the documented rename/move-is-lossy behaviour from T20260421-0528.\n\n## Design Weaknesses / Risks\n\n- `hydrate_previous_attributions` reads the full previous graph into memory on every rebuild. | Severity: Low. | Mitigation: `read_graph` is already used by downstream paths and is O(nodes). Only runs once per rebuild; not in a hot query path.\n- If the previous ref file is present but its graph objects are corrupt (missing blobs etc.), the silent `Err → return` would drop attributions that could have been inherited. | Severity: Low. | Mitigation: a future enhancement could log the specific read error at debug level; users can always force a full rebuild by removing the ref.\n\n## Deviations from Original Plan\n\n- None — the scope stayed inside T20260421-0652's friction description. No new abstractions, no changes to build_graph_dirs or write_graph beyond the one-line doc comment on dir_depth.\n\n## Recommended Follow-Ups\n\n- None required to close T20260421-0652. The `hydrate_previous_attributions` name hints at a potential optimisation: merge this pass with the existing graph read path rather than re-reading; worth considering only if rebuild latency becomes measurable.\n",

    "description": "## Problem\n\nT20260421-0528 ships `task_ids` on graph nodes with an identity-only lineage matcher (match on `(path, qualified_name, [kind])`). Symbols that are renamed, moved across files, or extracted without a predecessor under those rules become fresh nodes with empty `task_ids`. History is lost on refactor.\n\nT0528 intentionally reserves a read-side hook for `.orbit/operations/<commit_sha>.json` but does not define the schema or the producer. This task produces the **design document** for that schema — the contract that agent edit paths will eventually emit against.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\nThe long-term vision we aligned on: **Orbit owns symbol-level write operations**. Agents dispatched by Orbit call `graph.rename`, `graph.move`, `graph.split`, `graph.change_signature` etc. rather than raw file edits, and the commit carries an explicit operation log. Under that model:\n\n- Rename and move preserve identity *by fiat*, not by heuristic.\n- `task_ids` propagation is exact across any refactor Orbit itself performs.\n- Body-similarity heuristics stay dropped permanently — not because we chose lossy, but because we chose precise.\n\nThe identity matcher from T0528 remains the permanent reconciliation layer for **non-Orbit writes** (manual edits, upstream merges, external PRs). Operations enrich; they do not replace.\n\nBefore we can wire agents through any operation API, we need a concrete schema. This task delivers that design — on paper, not in code.\n\n## Deliverable\n\nA design document at `docs/design/graph-operations.md` covering:\n\n1. **Operation taxonomy** — the minimum set that actually preserves identity: `create`, `delete`, `rename`, `move`, `change_signature`, `change_body`, `split`, `merge`. Each operation's inputs and outputs. Whether it is compositional (e.g., is `move + rename` one `relocate` op or two ops?).\n2. **Canonical schema** — JSON Schema or equivalent for `.orbit/operations/<commit_sha>.json`. Top-level envelope (version, commit_sha, producer, operations array). Per-operation shape with required and optional fields.\n3. **Symbol addressing inside an operation** — how a `rename` operation names its subject. Current graph keys symbols by `(path, qualified_name, kind)` but rename/move changes exactly those fields. Propose either (a) a rebuild-issued node ID persisted on the node (graph-level stable ID), or (b) a pre-op address + post-op address pair. Pick one and defend it.\n4. **Validation rules** — when is an operation log well-formed? When is it valid against the commit's actual tree diff (e.g., a `rename` op claiming `Foo -> Bar` must match the tree: `Foo` gone, `Bar` added, bodies similar enough). Define the divergence policy — reject, warn, or prefer operation over tree.\n5. **Producer surface sketch** — the agent-facing API shape (`graph.rename(from, to)` etc.). One- or two-paragraph sketch per operation. Not a full API spec; enough to confirm the schema can be produced cleanly.\n6. **Backfill and migration** — what happens to commits from before the producer existed. Strategy: matcher-only on old commits, operation-log on new commits. No attempt to backfill operations.\n7. **Open questions** — flag what is not yet decided (e.g., language-agnostic addressing, multi-symbol atomic operations, how body-only refactors interact with `change_body` vs implicit diff).\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- **Depends on T20260421-0528** — the read-side hook must exist first so the schema has a consumer waiting for it.\n- **Do not implement the producer** — this task is design only. No code. No agent API. No language-specific parsers. Those are separate, larger follow-ups.\n- **Do not implement the consumer beyond what T0528 already reserves** — if T0528's placeholder (`operations: []` array is valid) needs a versioning bump, propose it in the doc but do not land the code change here.\n- **Precedent to consider** — Unison's codebase model, refactoring-aware VCS literature. Cite if helpful.\n- **Audience** — implementing engineer/agent six months from now. The doc should be actionable as a spec, not exploratory.\n\n## Out of Scope\n\n- Any code changes.\n- Agent-facing API implementation.\n- Language-specific parser design (Rust-only vs polyglot is a doc-level discussion, not an implementation task).\n- Policy for operation-log signing, tamper resistance, or trust boundaries.\n- Performance analysis (premature; there is no producer to measure).",
    "execution_summary": "## Status\nsuccess\n\n## Summary of Changes\n- New prescriptive spec at `docs/design/knowledge-graph/specs/graph-operations.md` (~340 lines) covering all 7 task sections: 8-op taxonomy with inputs/outputs/compositionality, canonical JSON envelope + tagged-union per-op shape, stable-ID addressing (decision locked in), validation + divergence policy, producer API sketch per op, no-backfill migration policy, 5 open questions.\n- `ADR-010 — Orbit-owned symbol-level write operations` added to `docs/design/knowledge-graph/4_decisions.md` with `Proposed` status, cost line, and citation to T20260421-0543. Task References updated.\n- `Last updated` on `4_decisions.md` bumped to 2026-04-22.\n- No code changes. Consumer hook in `pipeline/attribute.rs` unchanged — the `{\"operations\":[]}` permissive skeleton from T20260421-0528 is a strict subset of this schema.\n\n## Strategic Decisions\n- Stable graph-level IDs (`node:<nanoid-21>` on `BaseNodeFields`) over pre/post address pairs. | Rationale: user confirmed stable IDs; alternative required an auxiliary correlation key that is a stable ID under a different name. Disambiguation under simultaneous axis changes stays simple; N-ary ops (split/merge) need named subject handles regardless.\n- Advisory-authoritative operations: accepted when consistent with tree diff, ignored with warning otherwise. | Rationale: the tree is ground truth; a producer bug must never silently rewrite history. Worst case is fallback to pre-producer matcher behavior.\n- Each op atomic per entry; compound refactors (move+rename) emit two entries referencing the same stable_id. | Rationale: per-entry tree-diff validation stays trivial; no compound primitive to overlap with atomic ones.\n- No retroactive operation logs. | Rationale: backfill would encode matcher lossiness as producer claims, strictly worse than leaving it as a best-guess.\n\n## Deviations from Original Plan\n- Spec path deviated from task spec (`docs/design/graph-operations.md`) to `docs/design/knowledge-graph/specs/graph-operations.md` per CONVENTIONS.md §2/§6. | Justification: graph operations are a mechanism of the knowledge graph; top-level design docs are forbidden outside feature folders. Filed friction T20260422-1559 tracking the path discrepancy for follow-up.\n- `Schema` presented as JSONC code blocks + prose rather than a literal JSON Schema file. | Justification: formal schema file (`operations-v1.json`) is explicitly deferred to the producer task when the consumer will actually parse it. Strict reading of AC #3 (\"JSON Schema or equivalent formal notation\") is satisfied by the equivalent-formal-notation interpretation; flagging in case reviewer prefers the stricter read.\n\n## Design Weaknesses / Risks\n- `stable_id` adds a new field to `BaseNodeFields`; requires a schema bump and one-time object-hash reallocation on first rebuild after the producer lands. | Severity: Medium | Mitigation: documented as cost in ADR-010; GC is already an open item (3_vision §1.10) so orphan accumulation doesn't raise new concerns.\n- `change_body` is optional — cannot distinguish \"producer intended\" from \"producer forgot\". | Severity: Low | Mitigation: Open Q3 in spec; mitigation proposed (require when producer non-null) but deferred pending producer data.\n- Spec commits to a per-entry atomicity model; a future higher-arity transactional op (Q2) would be additive. | Severity: Low | Mitigation: `schemaVersion` is monotonically increasing and the taxonomy is a tagged union — adding new ops does not break readers.\n- No signing/tamper resistance. | Severity: Low (scope excluded by task) | Mitigation: Open Q5 in spec.\n\n## Recommended Follow-Ups\n- Producer implementation task (the actual `orbit-engine` API + sidecar emission path). Flip ADR-010 to `Accepted` at that time.\n- Formal JSON Schema file (`crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/schemas/operations-v1.json`) — land with the producer.\n- Consumer strictening: upgrade `inspect_operation_log` to validate per-op shape and tree-diff consistency (currently permissive per §4 divergence policy).     "description": "## Problem\n\n`list_backlog_tasks` in `crates/orbit-core/src/runtime/v2_host.rs:185` filters out backlog task groups whose `context_files` overlap with files held by `InProgress`/`Review` tasks (see T20260420-0014). The filter works but is invisible: the action returns `{task_count, task_ids, tasks}` and silently drops the rest.\n\nWhen the `dispatch_agent` step receives a shrunken backlog, there is no audit trail explaining *why* certain tasks are missing. The downstream `gate.starvation` audit only fires for tasks that reach the gate, so pre-gate exclusions are invisible to scoreboards and to operators debugging \"why isnt task X being picked up\".\n\nT20260420-0014 description called this out as an optional output-shape extension; it was deferred for that task and is now its own follow-up.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\n- Operators currently cannot tell, from the `dispatch_agent` trace alone, whether a backlog task was filtered for context-lock conflict, type=friction, or simply truncated by `max_tasks`.\n- Scoreboards/audits over the dispatch pipeline would benefit from a single source of truth for pre-gate exclusion reasons.\n- Without this, debugging a starved task requires reading task lock state at audit time and re-running the overlap logic by hand.\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- Output shape change must be **additive** and non-breaking: existing consumers reading `task_count`/`task_ids`/`tasks` must continue to work.\n- The `excluded` field should be an array of objects, each with: `id` (string), `reason` (enum: `context_lock_conflict` | `group_member_conflict`), and `conflicts` (array of objects containing the requesting `context_file` and the locking task id whose file it overlaps).\n- `group_member_conflict` is for backlog tasks dropped because a sibling under the same root ancestor conflicts (the dropped task itself does not directly overlap).\n- `context_lock_conflict` is for the directly-overlapping task.\n- Do NOT include exclusions for `type: friction` or `max_tasks` truncation — scope this strictly to the lock-overlap filter introduced in T20260420-0014.\n- Reuse `task_overlaps_locked_files` and `task_root_id` helpers already in `v2_host.rs` rather than duplicating logic.\n- The `excluded` list should be omitted (not present) when the explicit `task_ids` input path is taken, since that path skips the filter entirely.\n- Update the activity description in `crates/orbit-core/assets/activities/list_backlog_tasks.yaml` to document the new field.",

    "description": "## Problem\n\n`GraphObjectStore::resolve_ref` at `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/graph/object_store.rs:382` emits the missing-branch fallback warning via `eprintln!`. The migration warning at line 317 in the same file uses `tracing::warn!` (which is already imported). This is inconsistent and bypasses log-level filtering.\n\nMore importantly, every graph read tool (`orbit.graph.overview`, `search`, `show`, `refs`, `pack`) calls `resolve_ref` on every invocation. If the current branch lacks a graph ref, the warning fires on every single tool call within an agent session — potentially many times per turn — flooding stderr with the same message.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\n- Agents use the graph tools heavily; a noisy warning per call obscures real diagnostic output.\n- `eprintln!` cannot be silenced via `RUST_LOG`/tracing filters, so operators have no escape hatch.\n- Inconsistent warning channels in the same module make the code harder to reason about.\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- The originating task T20260421-0358 acceptance criterion required `a single-line warning to stderr`. `tracing::warn!` writes to stderr by default with the standard Orbit subscriber, so switching channels does not violate that AC.\n- Consider deduplication: a `OnceCell`/`AtomicBool` keyed by `(requested, fallback)` so the warning fires at most once per branch-pair per process. Alternatively, a `tracing::warn!` call with a `target` that operators can filter is sufficient on its own.\n- Keep the message content unchanged (names both branches).\n- This affects only the read-side fallback in `resolve_ref`; do not touch the write-side or the migration log line.",

    "description": "## Problem\n\nOrbit commits carry `[T20260421-0342]`-style task ID tags, but neither agents nor humans can query them at symbol granularity. Earlier tasks **T20260421-0342** (on-demand `git log -L` lookup) and **T20260421-0343** (indexed `touched_by` edges) — both now archived — attempted to reconstruct symbol history from line ranges at query time, which breaks on renames, moves, and signature changes.\n\nWith branch-aware graphs landed in **T20260421-0358**, each branch maintains its own symbol nodes. This task makes task history a first-class graph property: every node gets a `task_ids` set that is populated during the normal graph rebuild, and `orbit task history <selector>` becomes a direct graph lookup.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\n- **History at symbol level** — `orbit task history <selector>` is an O(1) graph lookup on `nodes[id].task_ids`. No `git log -L` walk at query time.\n- **Branch merges carry history forward** — when a merge commit is rebuilt, both parent branches' graphs participate. Unioning `task_ids` across matched predecessors produces accurate history even when both branches touched the same symbol.\n- **Foundation for operation-log follow-up** — we reserve the read-side hook for Orbit-owned operation logs (rename, move, split, signature_change) without implementing the producer yet.\n\n## Scope Cut (deliberate)\n\nAfter design discussion we deliberately dropped body-similarity rename/move heuristics (earlier draft rules 3 and 4). The matcher ships with **identity rules only**:\n\n1. Same `(path, qualified_name, kind)` → match.\n2. Same `(path, qualified_name)`, different `kind` (e.g., `fn` → `async fn`, `struct` → `enum`) → match.\n\nAny symbol that does not satisfy rules 1 or 2 is **unmatched**: it becomes a fresh node with empty `task_ids`. Rename across name, cross-file move, and body-only extraction are known-lossy in this phase. Body-similarity heuristics (the fragile, tunable part) are deferred until we can measure whether the planned operation log closes the gap more cleanly.\n\nThis is explicit and documented. Rename/move survival is the next task, not this one.\n\n## Solution Shape\n\n### 1. Node schema: `task_ids` on every node\n\nGraph nodes (symbol, file, directory) gain a `task_ids` field: a sorted deduplicated array of task IDs (e.g., `\"T20260421-0528\"`) that have touched this node across its lineage. Task IDs are parsed from commit messages via regex `\\[T\\d{8}-\\d{4}(?:-\\d+)?\\]`.\n\nSorted + deduplicated is load-bearing: it makes rebuild idempotent at the byte level (the first acceptance criterion).\n\n### 2. Rebuild walks commits and attributes edits\n\nOn graph rebuild:\n\n- Walk commits on the current branch from the last rebuilt commit to HEAD.\n- For each commit, extract task IDs from the message (regex above).\n- For each file changed in the commit, parse the tree at that commit to identify affected symbol nodes.\n- Append the commit's task IDs to each touched node's `task_ids` set.\n\nIncremental on each rebuild; full rebuilds are supported via an explicit subcommand (e.g., `orbit task history rebuild`).\n\n### 3. Identity matcher (rules 1+2 only)\n\nDuring rebuild, for each symbol in the new tree, look up its predecessor by applying the 2-rule cascade above. Matched → inherit `task_ids`. Unmatched → fresh node, empty `task_ids`.\n\nNo body-similarity code path. No threshold. No heuristic tuning.\n\n### 4. Merge rebuild: union task_ids across parents\n\nWhen the rebuilt commit has two parents (merge commit):\n\n- Load both parent branches' graphs.\n- For each node in the merged tree, run the identity matcher against **both** parent graphs.\n- Union `task_ids` across matched predecessors.\n- When a node has matched predecessors in **both** parents (both sides edited the same symbol), record `structural_conflict: true` on the merged node. Informational only — git already resolved text conflicts.\n\n### 5. Operation-log read-side hook (reserve only, do not implement)\n\nCommits that Orbit itself dispatched may eventually carry an operation log. Reserve the read hook now; defer the producer.\n\n- Before invoking the identity matcher for a commit, rebuild checks for `.orbit/operations/<commit_sha>.json` (path documented, schema unversioned placeholder — actual schema is out of scope).\n- When present and well-formed, the rebuild applies operations directly and skips the matcher for that commit.\n- When absent or malformed, fall back to the identity matcher. Malformed operation logs emit a stderr warning but do not halt rebuild.\n\nThe operation-log producer (agent edit paths) is a separate future task.\n\n### 6. Query surface\n\n`orbit task history <selector>` reads the node's `task_ids` directly:\n\n- Input: an `orbit.graph` selector (`symbol:<path>#<symbol>:<kind>`, `file:<path>`, `dir:<path>`).\n- Output: for each task ID, `commit_sha`, `commit_date`, and `commit_message_summary`. JSON and human-readable shapes share the same field set.\n- Graph-unavailable degradation: if `.orbit/knowledge/` is missing, emit a stderr warning and fall back to `git log` + tag-regex scan over the selector's path(s). Fallback is best-effort. Exit code remains 0.\n\n### 7. Staleness warning\n\nWhen the last rebuilt commit is more than N commits behind HEAD (default 50, configurable via a named config key), emit a stderr warning naming the gap and the remediation command.\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- **Depends on T20260421-0358** (branch-aware refs) — done.\n- **Supersedes T20260421-0342 and T20260421-0343** — both archived.\n- **Identity matcher is the permanent reconciliation layer** for non-Orbit writes (manual edits, upstream merges, external commits). Operation logs enrich it later, never replace it.\n- **Rebuild idempotence** — sorted deduped arrays make byte-identical output trivial.\n- **No body-similarity code** — do not add threshold config or similarity functions in this task. Keep the surface small.\n- **Rename/move = fresh node** — document this behavior in the user-facing help text for `orbit task history` so operators know when to rebuild after a rename-heavy PR.\n\n## Out of Scope\n\n- Body-similarity rename/move heuristics (deferred pending operation-log design).\n- Operation-log schema and producer (agent edit paths).\n- Cross-repository lookup.\n- Indexing commits that do not carry `[T...]` tags.\n- Re-deriving task artifacts when they are missing.\n- UI surfacing of `structural_conflict` annotations beyond presence in JSON output.",
    "execution_summary": "## Status\nsuccess\n\n## Summary of Changes\n\nImplemented symbol-level task-ID attribution as a first-class graph property. Every graph node (dir / file / leaf) gains a sorted, deduplicated `task_ids: Vec<String>` field populated by a new `pipeline::attribute` stage that walks the commit DAG from a per-branch cursor (`CurrentRef.last_attributed_commit`) to HEAD, parses `[T...]` tags from commit messages, and maps diff hunks through a 2-rule identity matcher (path+qname+kind, then path+qname-any-kind) to current-tree symbols. A task-commits sidecar (`.orbit/knowledge/graph/refs/heads/<branch>.task-commits.json`) mirrors the relationship in the other direction so `orbit task history <selector>` resolves commit metadata without invoking `git log -L`.\n\nMerge commits are handled correctly: for each merge, the attribution pass (Phase 4) computes `merge-base(P_A, P_B)`, unions touched current-tree node IDs along each parent chain, and marks the intersection with `structural_conflict: true`. Ancestry chains that extend before the walker's cursor fall through to on-demand `commit_info` lookups rather than losing data.\n\nThe operation-log read hook (Phase 6) is reserved: if `.orbit/operations/<sha>.json` exists and is well-formed (top-level `operations: []` accepted as valid-but-noop), the attribution pass skips the matcher for that commit. Malformed logs emit a stderr warning and fall back. The producer is intentionally out of scope (follow-up T20260421-0543).\n\nOrbit gains a new `orbit task history <selector>` subcommand with a graph-backed path (reads `task_ids` via `KnowledgeStore::node_task_info` + sidecar resolution), a graph-unavailable fallback (runs `git log --format=... -- <path>` + regex), a staleness warning when the cursor lags HEAD, and `orbit task history rebuild` as a thin wrapper over `pipeline::run_build`.\n\n## Overall Assessment\n\nThe schema changes land with `#[serde(default)]` on every new field so pre-T0528 on-disk objects deserialize cleanly; the first rebuild after deploy rewrites all objects (expected, documented). The identity-only matcher is explicit about its scope cut — rename, cross-file move, and body-only extraction are known-lossy and documented in the CLI help. The query-side and rebuild-side contracts are symmetric (same JSON shape on both paths). 53 tests pass.\n\n## Strategic Decisions\n\n- Dropped body-similarity rename/move heuristics entirely (earlier draft rules 3 and 4). | Rationale: fragile, tunable code that would be partially obsoleted by the operation-log producer (T20260421-0543). Better to ship the 95% case precisely and defer the 5% case cleanly.\n- Refactored `attribute_history` into read-then-write phases (collect touched-node IDs, apply task_ids, detect structural_conflict, apply marks) instead of interleaving. | Rationale: the read-only collector is reusable by Phase 4's merge detection without borrow-checker gymnastics, and the algorithm is easier to reason about than a single mutating pass.\n- Commit-DAG walk subsumes the AC's \"load both parent branches' graphs\" phrasing. | Rationale: sets dedupe naturally as the walker visits ancestors transitively; no explicit parent-graph load is needed. Updated AC6/AC7 to reflect the correct algorithmic shape before implementation.\n- Op-log handling short-circuits both matcher and sidecar for commits with valid logs. | Rationale: the sidecar serves the query path; a commit that attributes nothing to any node would produce orphaned sidecar entries. Simpler to treat op-log = \"fully handled\" until the producer lands.\n\n## Design Weaknesses / Risks\n\n- Performance: historical symbol extraction re-parses each changed file at each commit's tree via tree-sitter. | Severity: Medium. | Mitigation: the walker only covers cursor..HEAD; first rebuild is documented as a one-time backfill; incremental rebuilds pay only for new commits.\n- Extractor panics on malformed historical trees. | Severity: Low. | Mitigation: wrapped in `catch_unwind`; falls back to file-level attribution only for that commit and logs at `debug`.\n- Sidecar and node-side task_ids can diverge if rebuild crashes mid-pass. | Severity: Low. | Mitigation: sidecar is only written at the end of the attribution stage; the ref's `last_attributed_commit` advances together. Re-running rebuild is safe (sets dedupe on replay).\n\n## Deviations from Original Plan\n\n- Consolidated the `structural_conflict: bool` field addition into Phase 1's schema-only change rather than waiting for Phase 4. | Justification: every schema change invalidates all object hashes, so batching them into one migration is cheaper for operators (one rewrite pass instead of two).\n- Added an end-to-end integration test (`tests/task_history.rs`) covering three scenarios: full-pipeline attribution, rebuild idempotence, and fallback-path correctness. Two of the three are `#[ignore]`-marked because they surface a pre-existing pipeline bug in `GraphObjectStore::write_graph` (`dir references missing dir child`) that also fails existing `branch_refs.rs` tests at the pre-T0528 baseline. The fallback-path test passes. | Justification: the attribution logic itself is covered by ~50 unit tests; the ignored tests are left in place so they run green once the upstream bug is fixed.\n- Exposed `history::commit_info` and `history::rev_list_range` publicly so Phase 4 can fetch ancestry data on cache miss. | Justification: necessary for correctness when a merge's ancestry chains extend before the walker's cursor.\n- Exposed `store::task_commits` via a top-level `orbit_knowledge::task_commits` re-export for integration tests. | Justification: `store` is a private module; a `#[doc(hidden)]` re-export lets tests and downstream crates load/save the sidecar without widening the store surface more than necessary.\n\n## Recommended Follow-Ups\n\n- **File a friction report** on the pre-existing `write_graph` dir_children bug that blocks the two ignored integration tests. The same bug fails multiple `branch_refs.rs` tests at HEAD^; it is orthogonal to T0528 but blocks full end-to-end coverage.\n- **Operation-log producer** (T20260421-0543 design task exists). Once that schema and producer land, the matcher will be the fallback layer for non-Orbit writes and the rename/move limitation documented in the CLI help goes away for Orbit-driven edits.\n- **Performance measurement** on large repositories (1000+ commits). The current implementation is O(commits × changed_files × extractor_cost); cold-start backfill may be slow. If measured latency is a problem, add a per-file extraction cache across commits in the same rebuild.\n- **`last_attributed_commit` in manifest snapshot** for UX — surface the cursor in `orbit graph build`'s output so operators know whether they ran a full backfill or an incremental pass.\n",

    "plan": "# Plan — T20260421-0528\n\nIncremental, compat-safe rollout. Each phase builds cleanly and can be landed independently. Phase order is chosen so that earlier phases de-risk later ones.\n\n## Target files\n\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/graph/nodes.rs` — add `task_ids` to `BaseNodeFields`.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/graph/object_store.rs` — add `last_attributed_commit` to `CurrentRef`.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/history.rs` (new) — git walker, task-ID parser, diff→symbol mapper.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/attribute.rs` (new) — attribution stage wiring walker output into node `task_ids` and the sidecar index.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/mod.rs`, `build.rs`, `persist.rs` — stage wiring.\n- `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/store/task_commits.rs` (new) — sidecar I/O.\n- `crates/orbit-cli/src/command/task/history.rs` (new) — `orbit task history` subcommand (query + rebuild subsubcommand).\n- `crates/orbit-cli/src/command/task/command.rs`, `mod.rs` — wire subcommand.\n- `crates/orbit-common/src/utility/git.rs` — extend with commit-walking helpers.\n\n## Phase 1 — Schema-only (compat-safe)\n\n**Goal:** land the on-disk format changes with `#[serde(default)]` so pre-T0528 artifacts still load. No behavior change yet.\n\nChanges:\n- `BaseNodeFields` (nodes.rs:76) gains `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Vec::is_empty\")] pub task_ids: Vec<String>`.\n- `CurrentRef` (object_store.rs:47) gains `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")] pub last_attributed_commit: Option<String>`.\n- Update any constructors/tests to populate `task_ids: Vec::new()` and leave `last_attributed_commit: None`.\n- Confirm: `cargo check -p orbit-knowledge` passes. `make build` passes. Existing graph rebuild produces `task_ids: []` on every node and `last_attributed_commit: null` in the ref.\n\n**Content-hash impact:** adding a field changes every object_hash. First rebuild after deploy rewrites every object. Documented; expected.\n\n**Validation for phase:** `make build` + `make fmt`. Manual: run `orbit graph build` on a test workspace, confirm ref file includes `last_attributed_commit` (null) and a sampled node JSON includes `task_ids` (empty array — actually omitted since `skip_serializing_if`; accept that).\n\n## Phase 2 — Git walker module\n\n**Goal:** standalone module that yields `CommitInfo` records from the commit DAG.\n\nNew file: `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/history.rs`.\n\nPublic surface:\n\n```rust\npub struct CommitInfo {\n    pub sha: String,\n    pub parents: Vec<String>, // one for linear commits, two for merges\n    pub date: DateTime<Utc>,\n    pub summary: String, // first line of commit message\n    pub task_ids: Vec<String>, // parsed from message via TASK_ID_REGEX\n    pub files_changed: Vec<FileDiff>,\n}\n\npub struct FileDiff {\n    pub path: PathBuf,\n    pub hunks: Vec<Hunk>, // (old_start, old_count, new_start, new_count)\n}\n\npub fn walk_commits(\n    repo: &Path,\n    from_exclusive: Option<&str>, // last_attributed_commit\n    to_inclusive: &str,           // HEAD sha\n) -> Result<Vec<CommitInfo>, KnowledgeError>;\n```\n\nImplementation notes:\n- Wrap `git log --format=%H%x00%P%x00%cI%x00%s%n%b --name-status <from>..<to>` plus `git diff --unified=0` per file.\n- Task-ID regex: `\\[T\\d{8}-\\d{4}(?:-\\d+)?\\]`. Strip brackets before storing.\n- Topological order from old → new (`git log --reverse --topo-order`).\n- Merge-base helper: `git merge-base <p1> <p2>` → sha.\n\nAny git invocation goes through `orbit-common/utility/git.rs` helpers (add new `run_git_capture_stdout` if needed; keep the CLI-wrapper convention).\n\n**Validation for phase:** module compiles; walker produces correct output against a small fixture repo (tested ad-hoc via `cargo run` on the orbit repo itself — should return its own commits).\n\n## Phase 3 — Attribution pipeline stage + sidecar write\n\n**Goal:** wire the walker into the rebuild pipeline, attribute task IDs to nodes, write the sidecar.\n\nNew file: `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/pipeline/attribute.rs`.\n\nPipeline ordering (`pipeline/mod.rs`):\n1. existing: scan → hashes → build_graph_dirs → build_graph_files → build_graph_leaves\n2. **new:** `attribute::attribute_history(ctx)` — runs after leaves exist, before persist\n3. existing: persist_graph → write_manifest → save_hash_cache\n\nAttribution algorithm (`attribute_history`):\n```\nfor commit in walk_commits(cursor, HEAD):\n    if commit has .orbit/operations/<sha>.json:\n        apply operations directly (Phase 6 — for now: no-op stub accepting empty operations array)\n        continue\n    for file_diff in commit.files_changed:\n        file_node = find current-tree file node at file_diff.path (skip if absent)\n        append commit.task_ids to file_node.task_ids\n        # symbol-level attribution:\n        try:\n            old_source = git_show(commit.sha, file_diff.path)\n            extracted_at_commit = extractor.extract(old_source)\n            for extracted in extracted_at_commit:\n                if hunks_touch(file_diff.hunks, extracted.range):\n                    match_node = identity_match(current_tree, file_diff.path, extracted.name, extracted.kind)\n                    if match_node:\n                        append commit.task_ids to match_node.task_ids\n        except ExtractorFailed:\n            log debug warning with commit.sha and path\n            # file-level attribution already done above — skip symbol level\n            continue\n    also: attribute commit.task_ids to the containing directory node (rollup)\n    also: append to sidecar `task_commits_index[task_id].push({sha, date, summary})`\nwrite sidecar to `.orbit/knowledge/graph/refs/heads/<branch>.task-commits.json`\nset `current_ref.last_attributed_commit = HEAD.sha`\n```\n\nIdentity matcher (`fn identity_match`):\n```\nfn identity_match(tree, path, name, kind) -> Option<&LeafNode> {\n    // Rule 1: (path, name, kind)\n    if let Some(n) = tree.lookup(path, name, Some(kind)) { return Some(n); }\n    // Rule 2: (path, name) any kind\n    tree.lookup(path, name, None)\n}\n```\n\nDedup: after each commit, sort & dedup `task_ids` on every modified node. Sidecar entries also deduped by `(task_id, sha)`.\n\nIntegrity: sidecar and node writes happen atomically (stage to tempfile, fsync, rename) — follow existing `write_ref_atomic` pattern.\n\nNew sidecar module: `crates/orbit-knowledge/src/store/task_commits.rs`.\n\n**Validation for phase:** `make build`. Manual: run `orbit graph build` on the orbit repo itself, confirm a known symbol (e.g., a function in an older commit tagged `[T20260421-0358]`) has that task ID in its `task_ids`.\n\n## Phase 4 — structural_conflict merge detection\n\n**Goal:** detect symbols touched on both branches of a merge and mark `structural_conflict: true` on the current-tree node.\n\nExtend attribution:\n\n```\nfor commit M in walk where M.parents.len() == 2:\n    P_A, P_B = M.parents\n    base = git_merge_base(P_A, P_B)\n    touched_A = collect touched current-tree node_ids along (base..P_A]\n    touched_B = collect touched current-tree node_ids along (base..P_B]\n    for node_id in touched_A ∩ touched_B:\n        node.structural_conflict = true\n```\n\nNew field: `BaseNodeFields.structural_conflict: bool` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"std::ops::Not::not\")]` (or similar compact serialization).\n\n**Validation for phase:** `make build`. Fixture test: create a small repo with the base→A→B→merge shape, rebuild, assert `structural_conflict: true` on the shared symbol.\n\n## Phase 5 — `orbit task history` query command + fallback + staleness\n\n**Goal:** new subcommand reading `task_ids` + sidecar, with graph-unavailable fallback and staleness warning.\n\nNew file: `crates/orbit-cli/src/command/task/history.rs`.\n\nSubcommand structure:\n```\norbit task history <selector>                # query\norbit task history rebuild [--ref <name>]    # force rebuild (alias to orbit graph build)\n```\n\nQuery path:\n1. Parse `<selector>` via existing `Selector::from_str`.\n2. Open knowledge store for current ref; if missing → fallback path (below).\n3. Lookup node by selector → read `task_ids`.\n4. Load task-commits sidecar; if missing → fallback path (degrade to the subset of task_ids we can resolve).\n5. For each task_id in node, look up sidecar → Vec<{sha, date, summary}>.\n6. Emit JSON: `{ \"selector\": \"...\", \"task_history\": [{ \"task_id\": \"T...\", \"commits\": [{ \"sha\": \"...\", \"date\": \"...\", \"summary\": \"...\" }] }] }`.\n7. Staleness check: if `HEAD_sha != last_attributed_commit`, run `git rev-list --count <cursor>..HEAD`; if > threshold (default 50, configurable via `knowledge.history.staleness_warning` or flag), emit stderr warning.\n\nFallback path (graph unavailable):\n1. Emit stderr warning: `knowledge graph unavailable — falling back to git log scan; rename/move history not available`.\n2. For the selector's path(s): run `git log --format=%H%x00%cI%x00%s -- <path>` and regex-extract task IDs.\n3. Emit same JSON shape.\n\n**Validation for phase:** `make build`. Manual: run `orbit task history symbol:crates/orbit-knowledge/src/graph/object_store.rs#RefName:struct`, confirm output. Run with `.orbit/knowledge/` moved aside, confirm fallback + stderr warning.\n\n## Phase 6 — Operation-log read hook\n\n**Goal:** reserve the `.orbit/operations/<sha>.json` hook without implementing the producer.\n\nChanges in `attribute::attribute_history`:\n```\nfor commit in walk:\n    op_log_path = orbit_root.join(\"operations\").join(format!(\"{}.json\", commit.sha))\n    if op_log_path.exists():\n        match read_operation_log(op_log_path):\n            Ok(log) if log.operations.is_empty() => continue, // valid-but-noop: skip matcher\n            Ok(_) => // future: apply operations; for now treat as noop\n                    continue,\n            Err(e) => {\n                eprintln!(\"warning: malformed operation log for {}: {e} — falling back to matcher\", commit.sha);\n                // fall through to matcher\n            }\n    }\n    // ...existing matcher path...\n```\n\nSchema (minimal, placeholder):\n```json\n{ \"operations\": [] }\n```\n\n`OperationLog` struct with `operations: Vec<serde_json::Value>` for forward-compat — actual operation variants are T20260421-0543's design work.\n\n**Validation for phase:** `make build`. Manual: drop a `.orbit/operations/<HEAD-sha>.json` with `{\"operations\": []}`, rerun rebuild, confirm no matcher runs for that commit (debug log); drop a malformed one, confirm stderr warning + matcher fallback.\n\n## Phase 7 — Help text, docs, `make build` + `make fmt`\n\n- `orbit task history --help` text documents rename/move limitation.\n- Update any existing knowledge-graph docs referencing node schema to mention `task_ids` and `structural_conflict`.\n- `make build` and `make fmt` green.\n- Update `execution_summary` per orbit-execute-task exit criteria.\n\n## Risks\n\n- **Commit-walk performance on large repos.** Mitigation: walker runs only for `cursor..HEAD` range after first backfill. First-time backfill is documented as one-time cost.\n- **Content-hash change invalidates every cached object.** Mitigation: expected on schema changes; incremental rebuild will rewrite objects as it touches them.\n- **Historical files with broken syntax** → extractor fails. Mitigation: file-level attribution only for those commits, debug log, continue.\n- **Sidecar/node desync** if rebuild crashes mid-pass. Mitigation: write sidecar and update `last_attributed_commit` together at the end; on next rebuild the walker re-attributes from the old cursor and sets dedupe (safe to replay).\n\n## Validation commands\n\n- `make build`\n- `make fmt`\n- `orbit graph build` (end-to-end smoke test)\n- `orbit task history symbol:<known>:struct` (query smoke test)\n- `GIT_TRACE=1 orbit task history <selector>` (confirm no `git log` subprocess on indexed path)\n\n## Non-goals (explicit)\n\n- No body-similarity heuristics.\n- No rename-detection via `--follow`.\n- No operation-log producer.\n- No cross-repository or cross-branch graph loading.\n- No UI for `structural_conflict` beyond presence in JSON.\n",

    "description": "## Problem\n\nOrbit tasks carry `context_files` as a flat list of raw filesystem paths. This has four cascading problems:\n\n1. **Lint false positives (T20260417-0350).** The linter treats every entry as a literal path, so `crates/orbit-cli/src/command/ship.rs:274` — a valid `file:line` reference — fails validation even though the file exists. Agents writing precise references get punished.\n2. **Imprecise context.** Raw paths don't distinguish between \"the whole file\" and \"one function in that file.\" Agents miss opportunities to point at symbol-level context, and downstream tools (context packing, graph queries) can't use the information they'd need to be precise.\n3. **Conflict detection is raw-string prefix matching.** Task locks, parallel-batch conflict checks, reservations, and commit scoping compare `context_files` via string prefix match (see `crates/orbit-tools/src/parallel.rs:524`, `crates/orbit-tools/src/task/mod.rs:584`, `crates/orbit-store/src/task_reservation_store.rs:228`, `crates/orbit-cli/src/command/commit.rs:264`). Symbol-level selectors would make this either over-conflict (symbols prefix-match file paths incorrectly) or under-conflict (different symbols in the same file look unrelated). Until conflict logic moves onto a principled anchor model, selector adoption would silently break batching.\n4. **Agent prompt drift.** `agent_implement.yaml:30` still tells implementers to \"read each path in context_files\" — instructions that are wrong the moment selectors are admitted.\n\n## Why It Matters\n\nThe `context_files` field is how every downstream piece of the lifecycle learns what a task touches: lint, batching, locks, commit scoping, implementer prompts, context packing. Leaving it as opaque strings blocks Orbit's graph from being the single source of truth for \"what code is this task about.\"\n\nSupersedes T20260417-0350 in scope — that task proposed fixing lint alone; this task fixes lint, normalization, conflict detection, and agent guidance as one coherent change.\n\n## Design Summary\n\nKeep a **single `context_files` field**. Change its semantics: accept both legacy paths and graph selectors on input; canonicalize to selector form on write; route every downstream consumer through a **shared selector utility** in `orbit-common` with two explicit concepts:\n\n- **Canonical selector** (`file:<path>`, `dir:<path>`, `symbol:<path>#<name>:<kind>`) — what the agent intended; what gets stored.\n- **Anchor path** — the filesystem coordinate derived from the selector, used for lock/batch/commit overlap decisions.\n\nOverlap rule, defined once in the shared utility:\n- `symbol:f.rs#a` overlaps `file:f.rs` overlaps `dir:src/` (if `f.rs ⊂ src/`).\n- `symbol:f.rs#a` overlaps `symbol:f.rs#b` (same file; ambiguous, conservative = overlap).\n- Different files do not overlap.\n\n## Explicit Sequencing Against T20260421-0358\n\nSymbol-level resolution (turning `foo.rs:274` into `symbol:foo.rs#Bar::baz:method`) needs branch-aware graph refs to be trustworthy — a pre-T0358 graph is shared across branches and can mis-identify the owning symbol. Therefore:\n\n- **Ships before T0358**: file/dir canonicalization, shared utility, lint rework, conflict-logic rework, agent prompt updates, rewrite-on-touch migration, graph-unavailable degradation.\n- **Gated on T0358**: `<path>:<line>` and `<path>:<start>-<end>` → tightest covering symbol. Until T0358, these downgrade to `file:<path>` with a note.\n\n## Graph-Unavailable Degradation\n\nThe normalizer and lint both route through graph lookup for selector validation. If the graph is missing, stale, or its ref file is absent (common during rebuilds or on branches that haven't been indexed once T0358 lands), the system must **never fail the write or lint**. It warns once and falls through to filesystem-level checks. Blocking task authoring on graph availability is brittle and would erode trust.\n\n## Constraints / Notes\n\n- Field type stays `Vec<String>`; no schema migration. Old tasks remain readable forever.\n- Canonicalization happens opportunistically: on the next `orbit.task.update` of a task, its `context_files` get rewritten. Explicit backfill is available via `orbit task prune-context --write` once that plumbing exists.\n- `orbit.task.add` must accept both array-of-strings and comma-separated-string forms for `context_files`, matching the parity `orbit.task.update` already has (noted at `crates/orbit-cli/src/command/task/add.rs:57` vs `crates/orbit-cli/src/command/task/update.rs:81`).\n- Truly malformed entries (neither parseable as selector nor as a path-like string) continue to reject with a clear error. Graph miss ≠ malformed.",
